zh.lifeSummary: 當Louis I d’ANJOU於1339年7月23日出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瓦爾-德-馬內時，他的父親Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France20歲，母親Bonne de Luxembourg24歲。他於1360年7月9日與Marie de BLOIS-CHATILLON在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易麥-吉洛易索馬爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1384年9月20日在義大利普格利亞巴里比格利享年45歲，埋葬在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易麥-吉洛易安格斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Louis I d’ANJOU于1339年7月23日出生在法国i萊-德-福朗斯瓦爾-德-馬內时，他的父亲Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France20岁，母亲Bonne de Luxembourg24岁。他于1360年7月9日与Marie de BLOIS-CHATILLON在法国帕斯 德 拉 洛易麥-吉洛易索馬爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1384年9月20日在意大利普格利亞巴里比格利享年45岁，埋葬在法国帕斯 德 拉 洛易麥-吉洛易安格斯。

nameMeanings.name: d’ANJOU
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, for someone from Anjou, a former province of western France (see Langevin ).

variants.name: Langevin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Andre, Henri, Jean-Louis, Pierre, Adelard, Emile, Gaston, Leodore, Lucien, Marcel.

French: habitational name, with fused definite article l', for an Angevin, i.e. for someone from Anjou, a former province of western France that was ruled by a count as an independent territory from the 10th century. The province became a property of the English Crown for fifty years at the end of the 12th century. Compare Longway and Lonsway .

History: Mathurin Langevin dit Lacroix from Le Lude in Sarthe, France, married Marie-Thérèse Martin in Quebec City, QC, in 1674. Michel Langevin from Paris, France, married Marguerite Guertin in Contrecoeur, QC, in 1714. Jean Bergevin dit Langevin from Angers in Maine-et-Loire, France, married Marie Piton in Quebec City, QC, in 1668. Charles Citoleux/Sitoleux dit Langevin from Chambellay in Maine-et-Loire, France, married Marie-Louise Harel in Montreal, QC, in 1739.

nameMeanings.name: Louis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hlōd ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’. It was very common in French royal and noble families. Louis I ( 778–840 ) was the son of Charlemagne, who ruled as both King of France and Holy Roman Emperor. Altogether, the name was borne by sixteen kings of France up to the French Revolution, in which Louis XVI perished. Louis XIV, ‘the Sun King’ ( 1638–1715 ), reigned for seventy-two years ( 1643–1715 ), presiding in the middle part of his reign over a period of unparalleled French power and prosperity. In modern times Louis is also found in the English-speaking world (usually pronounced ‘loo-ee’). In Britain the Anglicized form Lewis is rather more common, whereas in America the reverse is true.

variants.name: Lewie
meanings: Anglicized spelling of   Louis  , or a pet form of   Lewis .

variants.name: Louie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Lewie   or   Louis  , currently in fashion.

variants.name: Lewi
meanings: form, since the Middle Ages, of the French name Louis . In modern use it is also in part a transferred use of the surname derived from this given name, or from Anglicized forms of Gaelic Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh ’ or Welsh Llewelyn .

en.lifeSummary: When Louis I d’ANJOU was born on 23 July 1339, in Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, France, his father, Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France, was 20 and his mother, Bonne de Luxembourg, was 24. He married Marie de BLOIS-CHATILLON on 9 July 1360, in Saumur, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 20 September 1384, in Bisceglie, Bari, Puglia, Italy, at the age of 45, and was buried in Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Louis I d’ANJOU est né le 23 juillet 1339, à Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, France, son père, Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Bonne de Luxembourg, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Marie de BLOIS-CHATILLON le 9 juillet 1360, à Saumur, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 20 septembre 1384, à Bisceglie, Bari, Puglia, Italie, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterré à Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Louis I d’ANJOU am 23. Juli 1339 in Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France 20 und seine Mutter Bonne de Luxembourg 24. Er hat am 9. Juli 1360 in Saumur, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich Marie de BLOIS-CHATILLON geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 20. September 1384 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Bisceglie, Bari, Puglia, Italien und wurde in Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich begraben.

Lui sposato Marie de BLOIS-CHATILLON il 9 luglio 1360, nel Saumur, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 20 settembre 1384, nel Bisceglie, Bari, Puglia, Italia, all'età di 45 anni, e fu sepolto a Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Louis I d’ANJOUが1339年7月23日にフランスiレ-デ-フランスヴァル-デ-マルンで生まれた時，父Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de Franceは20歳，母Bonne de Luxembourgは24歳であった。1360年7月9日にフランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーメインエ-エト-ロイアーサウムルでMarie de BLOIS-CHATILLONと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1384年9月20日にイタリアプウグリアバーイビスセグリーで45歳で死去し，フランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーメインエ-エト-ロイアーアンジャーズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Louis I d’ANJOU(이)가 1339년 7월 23일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 발-데-마니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Bonne de Luxembourg(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1360년 7월 9일 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 소머에서 Marie de BLOIS-CHATILLON(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1384년 9월 20일 이탈리아 푸그리야 버리 비스스그리에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 앤그르스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Louis I d’ANJOU nasceu em 23 de julho de 1339, em Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Bonne de Luxembourg, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Marie de BLOIS-CHATILLON em 9 de julho de 1360, em Saumur, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 20 de setembro de 1384, em Bisceglie, Bari, Puglia, Itália, com 45 anos, e foi sepultado em Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Louis I d’ANJOU родился 23 июля 1339 г. в местности вал-де-марн, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Bonne de Luxembourg, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Marie de BLOIS-CHATILLON 9 июля 1360 г. в местности саумур, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 20 сентября 1384 г. в местности биссегли, бари, пугля, Италия в возрасте 45 лет и был похоронен в местности ангирс, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Louis I d’ANJOU nació el 23 de julio de 1339, en Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Bonne de Luxembourg, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Marie de BLOIS-CHATILLON el 9 de julio de 1360, en Saumur, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 20 de septiembre de 1384, en Bisceglie, Bari, Puglia, Italia, a la edad de 45 años, y fue sepultado en Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia.
